title: Foundation Words
description: Essential everyday vocabulary for IELTS bands 4.5-5.5 — the words that recur across environment, education, health, travel, and work topics in Reading, Listening, and Speaking.

cards.definition: to become larger in number or amount, or to make something larger
cards.example: The number of tourists visiting the city has increased significantly in recent years.
cards.quiz_prompt: The number of tourists visiting the city has ___ significantly in recent years.

cards.definition: to become smaller in number or amount, or to make something smaller
cards.example: The population of the village has decreased steadily since the factory closed.
cards.quiz_prompt: The population of the village has ___ steadily since the factory closed.

cards.definition: to become better, or to make something better
cards.example: Regular exercise can improve both physical and mental health.
cards.quiz_prompt: Regular exercise can ___ both physical and mental health.

cards.definition: to give someone something that they need
cards.example: The government provides free education for all children.
cards.quiz_prompt: The government ___ free education for all children.

cards.definition: to make or grow something
cards.example: This region produces most of the country's fruit and vegetables.

cards.definition: to make something smaller in size, amount, or degree
cards.example: The new policy aims to reduce traffic in the city centre.
cards.quiz_prompt: The new policy aims to ___ traffic in the city centre.

cards.definition: to keep someone or something safe from harm or damage
cards.example: Wearing a helmet helps protect cyclists from serious injury.
cards.quiz_prompt: Wearing a helmet helps ___ cyclists from serious injury.

cards.definition: the natural world of land, water, air, plants, and animals
cards.example: Plastic waste causes serious damage to the environment.

cards.definition: the process of teaching and learning, especially in schools
cards.example: Good education gives children better chances in life.

cards.definition: the condition of a person's body or mind
cards.example: Eating fresh vegetables every day is good for your health.

cards.definition: to go from one place to another, especially a long distance
cards.example: Many young people travel abroad to study or work.

cards.definition: vehicles used for moving people or goods from one place to another
cards.example: Public transport in the city is cheap and reliable.

cards.definition: damage caused to air, water, or land by harmful substances or waste
cards.example: Air pollution is a major problem in many large cities.

cards.definition: an advantage or a helpful effect that something has
cards.example: One benefit of working from home is saving time on travel.
cards.quiz_prompt: One ___ of working from home is saving time on travel.

cards.definition: a situation that causes difficulty and needs to be dealt with
cards.example: Traffic congestion is a growing problem in most major cities.

cards.definition: a way of solving a problem
cards.example: Recycling is one solution to the problem of household waste.
cards.quiz_prompt: Recycling is one ___ to the problem of household waste.

cards.definition: the group of people who officially control a country
cards.example: The government announced new rules to protect the environment.

cards.definition: all the people who live in a particular country, area, or place
cards.example: The population of the city has grown rapidly in the last decade.

cards.definition: a group of people who live in the same area or share the same interests
cards.example: The new park brings the whole community together.

cards.definition: the state of having paid work
cards.example: The new factory has created hundreds of jobs and increased employment in the area.
cards.quiz_prompt: The new factory has created hundreds of jobs and increased ___ in the area.

cards.definition: the state of not having a job, or the number of people without a job
cards.example: Unemployment rose sharply after the factory closed.

cards.definition: a fixed amount of money that someone earns for their work, usually paid monthly
cards.example: She was offered a higher salary at her new job.

cards.definition: the money that a person or family earns or receives regularly
cards.example: Many families rely on a single income to cover their living costs.
cards.quiz_prompt: Many families rely on a single ___ to cover their living costs.

cards.definition: the amount of money needed to buy or do something
cards.example: The cost of living has risen sharply in recent years.

cards.definition: costing a lot of money
cards.example: Housing in the city centre is far too expensive for most students.
cards.quiz_prompt: Housing in the city centre is far too ___ for most students.

cards.definition: how good or bad something is
cards.example: The quality of the food in this restaurant is excellent.

cards.definition: the work that a business or organisation does to help customers
cards.example: The hotel is known for its friendly, efficient service.

cards.definition: something that is made to be sold, usually in a factory
cards.example: The company launched a new product last month.

cards.definition: the production of goods in factories
cards.example: The car industry employs thousands of people in this region.

cards.definition: the practice of farming, including growing crops and keeping animals
cards.example: Agriculture remains the main source of income in rural areas.

cards.definition: scientific knowledge used in practical ways, especially in machines
cards.example: New technology has changed the way people communicate.

cards.definition: the sharing of information between people
cards.example: Good communication is essential in any successful team.

cards.definition: a custom or belief that has existed for a long time
cards.example: Many families still follow the tradition of eating together on Sundays.

cards.definition: belonging to the present time, using the newest methods or ideas
cards.example: The city is famous for its modern architecture.

cards.definition: to grow or change into a more advanced state
cards.example: The country has developed quickly since it joined the trade agreement.
cards.quiz_prompt: The country has ___ quickly since it joined the trade agreement.

cards.definition: the process of growing or improving
cards.example: The new road is an important part of the region's development.

cards.definition: the state of being extremely poor
cards.example: Millions of people around the world still live in poverty.
cards.quiz_prompt: Millions of people around the world still live in ___.

cards.definition: having a lot of money
cards.example: The wealthy suburbs of the city have much better schools.

cards.definition: an illness affecting a person, animal, or plant
cards.example: Better hygiene has helped to reduce the spread of disease.

cards.definition: a substance used to treat or prevent illness
cards.example: The doctor prescribed medicine to help with her cough.

cards.definition: a place where sick or injured people are treated
cards.example: He was taken to hospital after the accident.

cards.definition: medical care given to a patient for an illness or injury
cards.example: Early treatment greatly improves the chances of recovery.

cards.definition: physical activity done to stay healthy and fit
cards.example: Doctors recommend at least thirty minutes of exercise a day.
cards.quiz_prompt: Doctors recommend at least thirty minutes of ___ a day.

cards.definition: the food that a person usually eats
cards.example: A balanced diet includes plenty of fruit and vegetables.

cards.definition: a feeling of worry caused by a difficult situation
cards.example: Long working hours can cause a lot of stress.

cards.definition: to rest and stop worrying
cards.example: It's important to relax after a long day at work.
cards.quiz_prompt: It's important to ___ after a long day at work.

cards.definition: a period of time spent away from home for rest or enjoyment
cards.example: We're planning a family holiday to the coast this summer.

cards.definition: a person who travels to a place for pleasure
cards.example: Thousands of tourists visit the old town every summer.

cards.definition: the business of providing services for tourists
cards.example: Tourism is one of the country's main sources of income.

cards.definition: land outside cities and towns, with fields and farms
cards.example: Many people move to the countryside to enjoy a quieter life.

cards.definition: relating to a city or town
cards.example: Urban areas often have better access to public transport.

cards.definition: relating to the countryside rather than towns or cities
cards.example: Rural communities can struggle to access good healthcare.

cards.definition: an illegal act that can be punished by law
cards.example: Crime rates have fallen in the city over the past five years.

cards.definition: the condition of being protected from danger or harm
cards.example: The new traffic lights have improved road safety.

cards.definition: likely to cause harm or injury
cards.example: Riding a motorbike without a helmet is extremely dangerous.

cards.definition: the ability to do something well, learned through training or practice
cards.example: Learning a new language is a valuable skill for finding work abroad.

cards.definition: information and understanding that a person has
cards.example: A good teacher shares their knowledge in a way students can understand.

cards.definition: knowledge or skill gained from doing something over time
cards.example: The job requires at least two years of experience.

cards.definition: a chance to do something
cards.example: Studying abroad gave her the opportunity to learn a new language.

cards.definition: the series of jobs a person has in a particular area of work over their life
cards.example: She built a successful career in medicine.
